subject: [RAW Overdue] Expired Risk Acceptance & Waiver Form

messages.body:
Dear Requestor,

Entity: FPG-Fairprice
Asset Name: Signcloud Saas User access management
Risk Description: There is a lack of user access management by the respective business owner.
RAW ID: RAW-20240306_01-v1.0

You have an expired waiver for the above stated system. The expiry date was May 15, 2025. If the residual risks have been remediated, please provide evidences to Cyber Risk Team (cyberRisk@ntucenterprise.sg) to review for closure. If not remediated yet, please submit a new risk acceptance and waiver (RAW) form as soon as possible and kindly note that this email reminder will continue to be sent until the new RAW form has been approved.

Kindly perform the entire process of raising a RAW request within Gsuite ie. all comments and approvals should be done through google docs.

Please follow the instructions below when you are raising a RAW form:

Please make a copy of the RAW form template to your personal drive to work on. Do not download.
Once Section A of the form is completed, kindly inform the Cybersecurity Risk Team (cyberRisk@ntucenterprise.sg) to review if it's correctly filled before obtaining any sign-off. Please also share the form with Technology Governance and Compliance Team (techgrc@ntucenterprise.sg).
Full name or initials could be indicated as sign-off and each sign-off must be accompanied with an "Approved" or "Signed" comment.

Thank you
Cyber Risk Team

subject: A Little Better: Celebrating the Women in FPG who Lead with Resilience and Excellence

messages.body:
Download

Hi Fairmily!

In celebration of International Women’s Day, we shine the spotlight on branch managers Jennifer and Rachael, two of the many women in FPG who are Beating Their Best in their day-to-day work. 
Download
Download
Since joining FairPrice Group in 2020, Rachael has redefined what it means to Beat Your Best.

Her journey began during the pandemic, managing our Finest stores at Changi Airport Terminal 3 and Jewel Changi Airport. With closed borders and zero tourists, hitting sales targets seemed impossible.
Download
However, Rachael and her team refused to be deterred, coming up with bold ideas such as curating locally flavoured products and even promoting the store on their personal RedNote accounts to attract tourists. Within just six months of borders reopening, they turned the tide and achieved a positive operating result. 
For Rachael, the goal isn't just about hitting a number; it’s about constant growth. “I never just want to hit my targets,” she shared. “I want to stretch myself and my team to see how much more we can achieve this month, and how we can do even better next month.”
This drive for progress led her to champion the pilot of our first Smart Carts at FairPrice Finest Sengkang Grand Mall. While introducing new technology to customers came with its own hurdles, Rachael leaned into the challenge with excitement. She took immense pride in being at the forefront of retail innovation in Singapore, guiding her team through the transition with unwavering resolve.
Rachael credits her growth to a simple but powerful philosophy:
Download
Download
Throughout her 19-year career with FPG, Jennifer has consistently proven that no challenge is too big when approached with a 'can-do' spirit.
While managing FairPrice Xtra Sports Hub, Jennifer faced the arrival of a nearby competitor. Instead of backing down, she hit the ground running, collaborating with product, supplier and marketing teams to launch promotional activities such as a Tsukiji Market-inspired event. From live bluefin tuna cutting shows to exclusive seafood promotions, her strategy didn't just protect sales; it drove a 3.3% year-over-year increase.

Jennifer’s dedication to excellence also extends to how we treat our customers. 
Leading the Net Promoter Score (NPS) workstream for Hyper, she championed simple yet powerful changes to delight customers, like implementing "Good Morning" and "Thank You" greetings at store exits. By conducting weekly power briefings across branches to keep customer experience top of mind, she helped lift the overall NPS score from 38 to 45. 
Jennifer leads by example with her infectious energy and a 'team-first' mindset. From managing complex live events to rolling kimbap on the front lines, she is always ready to dive in and help. For her, success is a team sport fuelled by grit and a shared commitment to excellence.
Download
Download
Whether it's embracing growth opportunities or innovating new ways to improve the customer experience, Rachael and Jennifer demonstrate that when we strive for excellence, we can truly Make Every Day A Little Better for everyone around us.
Download
We are building a culture of Growth Mindset In Action, which will define how we work as we transform our organisation for the future.

Know a hero whom you want us to feature? Submit your request here.
Download

subject: Notes: “[Placeholder] Display campaigns” Mar 13, 2026

messages.body:
Notes from “[Placeholder] Display campaigns”
These notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.
Open meeting notes
The content was auto-generated on March 13, 2026, 3:27 PM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.
Summary
Display campaign operationalization discussion focused on sequencing packages and campaigns with needed technical changes.

Ad Package API Status
The original request for the SAP invoice API related to ad packages was deprioritized by APG, but technical information is still needed for scheduling package downloads. A direct Ad Package API is currently unavailable, so information will be provided on scheduling a download of package information into a bucket (S3 or GCS).

Sequencing Challenge Display Campaigns
The goal is to transition all campaigns to impression-based booking, eliminating fixed tenancy while maintaining the restriction of non-endemic campaigns from the first slot. Packages are prioritized upfront but cause sequencing difficulty, leading to a proposal to use campaign priority overrides as a potential solution.

Critical PCNT Limit Increase
A critical technical requirement for the end state is increasing the upper limit of the PCNT (number of banners returned) from 10 to at least 20, as the current limit is insufficient for the transition and end state. Inventory-level CPM overrides and new pacing options (including front load, back load, even, and ASAP) are confirmed upcoming features for campaigns.

Suggested next steps
	[Akshay Deshmukh] Update Email: Send follow-up email about the SAP invoice. Include details regarding scheduling package downloads to S3 or GCS buckets.
	[Nikhil Grover] Share Presentation: Send the meeting slides to Akshay Deshmukh.
	[Akshay Deshmukh] Review Priority: Check with The group if campaign priority can override package priority settings.
	[Akshay Deshmukh] Update Timeline: Update Nikhil Grover on Monday regarding the ad group feature release timeline.

messages.body:
Dear Mr. Bui,

Greetings from LGMS.

Thank you for having us on the NTUC Enterprise-2025 Web Service Penetration Testing for RMN APIs initiative. My name is Xiao Cong and I will be the main contact person for this project. 

Kindly find the scope of work of this project tabulated below for your reference. Kindly let me know if there are any discrepancies of the SOW. 

Web Service Penetration Testing (White Box)

Network accessibility: External
Testing Window: During Office Hour
Type of Web Service: Retrieving Banners and Tracking Ad Interactions
Number of API: 1 API
Number of Roles: Not more than 2 roles
Number of Credentials per Role: Not more than 2 user credentials
Number of Functions to be tested in the web service scope: Not more than 3 functions

Please let me know if a kick-off meeting is required on your end, and we will be happy to arrange for one. Meanwhile, kindly find the prerequisites below, as these details are needed to facilitate the PT activities. 

Testing timeline: [1 December 2025 - 5 December 2025? ]
API specification/definition files (postman collection, openAPI, .etc) 
2 credentials (admin role/user role)
API flow documentation (if any)

subject: Notes: “Discussion on RMN CMS for smart cart” Mar 13, 2026

messages.body:
Notes from “Discussion on RMN CMS for smart cart”
These notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.
Open meeting notes
The content was auto-generated on March 13, 2026, 11:48 AM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.
Summary
RMN Centralized CMS feasibility examined architectural needs with a focus on Osmos integration for smart card deployment.

Assess Centralized CMS Feasibility
The meeting aimed to assess the technical possibility of a centralized Content Management System for various touch points like smart card, utilizing current retail media network assets. Technical assessment is required quickly to define architecture before funding or resource commitments are made for the project.

Smart Card Integration Protocol
Discussion centered on integrating the smart card system, confirming that the preferred method is a push API from Osmos, which should act as the source of truth for content, rules, and instructions. The current smart card production CMS can receive media via HTTP URL retrieval or SFTP content push.

Handling Interactive and Offline Displays
It was agreed that interactive displays like smart card and I-POS should connect directly to Osmos for real-time ad serving, supporting native URLs for ad clicks. Offline mode is necessary for non-interactive screens, such as the smart card welcome screen, requiring specific API documentation for content caching and offline play capabilities.

Suggested next steps
	[Michael Bui] Define Architecture: Assess the technical possibility and feasibility of implementing a centralized Content Management System. Collaborate with Tina, Henry, and Lens to define the architectural diagram for system integration.
	[Henry Huang, Tina Zhu, Len Guo] Share API Specs: Prepare full API documentation and specifications for the Smart Card system. Share the details, including SFTP requirements and data availability, with Michael Bui for review.
	[Miguel Ho Xian Da] Create Chat Group: Create a technical chat group for Michael Bui, Henry, and Len using Google Chat.
	[Miguel Ho Xian Da, Michael Bui] Expedite Development: Highlight the need to expedite development of the Osmo system scheduling and device management capability. Discuss this prioritization with Brian and Sean.

subject: Re: Retail Media

messages.body:
Dear Accenture Team

For context, this year we are scaling Smart Carts, Digital Price Card and IPOS (our new POS) to more FP stores. These are additional touchpoints (beyond digital signage) that retail media can leverage. The current challenge is that each of these touchpoints has its own content management system, and we want to look for a solution for operation.

I have the following questions:-

1) Does Osmos have the capability to directly publish the content to these touchpoints via API integration, or other integration methods? Because these touchpoints have different screen specifications, a content library will be necessary.

2) If the above is not possible, is there any solution that Accenture can recommend?

Thank you.

cc: Dear RMN team, and Michael, pl chip in if required. tks.

With regards,
Miguel Ho

subject: [Action required] App passwords deprecation notice

messages.body:
App passwords deprecation reminder

You will need to update your app passwords to API tokens in order not to affect your current workflows
We're reaching out to remind you about the change with Bitbucket Cloud's app passwords, effective June 9th, 2026. After this date, all app passwords will stop functioning. Many Bitbucket Cloud users currently rely on app passwords for integrations. To enhance security, Atlassian is standardizing authentication methods across all products.
As part of this effort, we're replacing app passwords with API tokens. These offer a more secure, manageable, and standardized way to authenticate.
What you need to know:

•	Check if you're affected: go to App passwords under Access Management in your Personal Bitbucket settings to identify which integrations or scripts currently use them.

•	Take action: before June 9th, 2026, generate Bitbucket scoped API tokens, update your scripts or tools with the new tokens, and test your workflows.

•	Why it matters: API tokens provide finer control with scoped permissions and expiration dates, while also providing stronger security when compared to app passwords.

Moving forward, you can use API tokens for all your authentication needs. To learn more about managing API tokens, please review this documentation.
Thank you for being a valued member of the Bitbucket community!

Best regards,

subject: Important Updates to the Employee Code of Conduct

messages.body:
Download

Dear Colleagues, 

We wish to inform you that the Code of Conduct has been updated to reflect FPG’s commitment to protecting sensitive information and maintaining a high level of integrity across the Group.

To support our shared success and maintain the standards set, we would like to highlight important guidelines on Confidentiality, Social Media, and External Communications. For further details, please refer to Sections C and E of the Code of Conduct here.

Download

As part of your work, you may handle Confidential Information including trade secrets, intellectual property, personal data and more. We all play a part in keeping this data safe.

To keep our information secure, please remember:

Use it for its intended purpose: Only access data for legitimate business needs.

Keep it internal: Never share sensitive details with unauthorised parties or use them for personal gain.

Use approved tools: Avoid storing company data on personal cloud services or entering it into unapproved AI platforms.

Spot something wrong? If you lose a device or suspect a data leak, please don't wait. Report it immediately to your Manager and the Cybersecurity Team so we can support you and protect the business.

Download

Our reputation is built on the trust of our customers and the hard work of our teams. While it is great to share about your work online, it’s important to remember that our responsibility to protect FPG's reputation extends to social media platforms.

To help us maintain a secure and professional presence, please follow these guidelines:

Protect our workspace: Do not post photos, screenshots, or videos that show internal systems, dashboards, or background data. Even a casual "desk selfie" can inadvertently reveal sensitive information.

Separate personal from the professional: It is important to keep personal opinions distinct from work-related communications. If your social media profile mentions your affiliation with FPG, do ensure it’s clear that your views and opinions are your own.

Not sure if a post is okay? If you are ever uncertain about sharing something online or accidentally post something sensitive, please reach out to your Manager for guidance.

These updates help maintain a secure, ethical and professional workplace while protecting our key strategic assets.

If you have any queries, please raise a ticket and our Care Centre will be happy to assist you.

subject: Notes: “FPG : Onsite programmatic” Mar 12, 2026

messages.body:
Notes from “FPG : Onsite programmatic”
These notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.
Open meeting notes
The content was auto-generated on March 12, 2026, 4:56 PM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.
Summary
Programmatic ad delivery solutions were discussed with FPJ prioritizing flexible ad dimensions with the Osmos SDK not yet supporting required features for a Q2 end go-live date, prompting consideration of a temporary API integration.

Programmatic Ad Delivery Scope
The discussion centered on finalizing programmatic ad delivery via Osmos, focusing on rendering ads on the front end and accurately tracking impression and click events, including managing necessary consents and ad format guidelines. IAB sizes from programmatic demand were noted as a limitation, though the FPJ required a non-negotiable 2x1 dimension for customer experience.

Rendering and Tracking Approach
The preferred approach involves FPJ's internal implementation rendering the ads based on information returned by the API, with the upgraded Osmos SDK being used solely for tracking purposes. This method requires back-end changes by the FPJ team and addressing the complexity of programmatic tracking, including compliance with MRCC and third-party verification tags.

SDK Readiness and API Alternative
The Osmos SDK upgrade is not ready, potentially picking up in Q2, prompting a request for an effort estimate for a temporary API integration alternative to support programmatic ads now. Programmatic deals can be managed as either advertiser-controlled or publisher-controlled, with FPJ anticipating using both scenarios.

Suggested next steps
	[Rahul Jain] Assess Integration Effort: Determine SDK readiness ETA. Provide effort estimate guidance for API integration path.
	[Rahul Jain] Send MRCC Documentation: Share documentation detailing tracking parameters, impression expectations, and MRCC guidelines.
	[Nikhil Grover] Finalize Commercial Timeline: Check and provide tentative timeline for commercial contracts signing and expected go-live date by Monday.
	[Nikhil Grover] Verify Google Account: Check internally regarding readiness for a live Google account (GAM or DB3) to accelerate validation timeline.
	Notes by Gemini

subject: GCP Service Account Key clean UP

messages.body:
Dear All,

As you are aware, we have automated service account key rotation in place for GCP. However, this process does not include many legacy service accounts. I've attached a spreadsheet listing these SAs, which either have keys that were never rotated or have more than two keys.

Please review the list and indicate your consent to include these SAs in the key rotation process by filling in the relevant information within the spreadsheet.

Thank you for your time on this matter.

GCP SA with Key

Regards,
Mohit Niranwal

Hi Jazz

Thank you for your support so far in helping us address the service account key concern. I would like to draw your attention to the service account key decommissioning activity for the DPD projects.

Based on our latest review, we have identified several service accounts that are still holding legacy service account keys. As part of our security best practices, these outdated keys need to be removed from the GCP projects. A similar practice is currently being implemented across other projects as well to ensure consistent security standards.

We have gathered the relevant information and attached a spreadsheet listing the service accounts that either have keys that were never rotated or currently have more than two active keys. This information is provided to obtain confirmation on including these service accounts in our automated key rotation process.

To proceed with the clean-up and decommissioning activity, could you please let us know the appropriate contact person whom we may coordinate with for this task? We are planning to schedule a discussion on Friday, 13th March, to review the remaining items and agree on the next steps.

Your support in helping us complete this security improvement initiative would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Regards
Himal Hewagamage

subject: Notes: “ACNxOsmos: Daily Cadence” Mar 12, 2026

messages.body:
Notes from “ACNxOsmos: Daily Cadence”
These notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.
Open meeting notes
The content was auto-generated on March 12, 2026, 2:06 PM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.
Summary
Segment and Osmos event count discrepancies and PLA targeting complexities were discussed, with app overhaul integration planning finalized for September.

Segment Osmos Event Discrepancy
The team confirmed that the difference in event counts between Segment and Osmos is due to unclarified destination function handling, with the recorded number representing all raw events received by Osmos. A collaborative working session was scheduled for the following day to test which events trigger to Osmos using specific user and SKU IDs.

Roadmap Prioritization and Updates
Q2 roadmap prioritization and timelines will be finalized in a meeting the following day. The PLA targeting update will not be delivered until after April as solutioning is ongoing, causing anxiety due to the business need to shift away from the fixed tendency model.

New App Overhaul Integration
The app overhaul is planned for the 1st week of September, with the major campaign launch on September 9. A full integration plan must be ready by mid-next month to ensure a scalable and modular integration using the right set of APIs.

Suggested next steps
	[Nikhil Grover] Schedule Testing: Set up working session for Segment testing tomorrow, 10:00 to 11:00 IST. Include Rahul Jain and Michael Bui in the meeting invite.
	[Rahul Jain] Add Attendees: Add necessary technology personnel to the Segment testing meeting invite.
	[Rahul Jain] Update Meeting Time: Speak with Akshai after the call; confirm timing for Friday display campaigns discussion. Update Nikhil Grover via WhatsApp with the confirmed time.
	[Nikhil Grover] Send Placeholder: Send Akshai a placeholder invite for the 12:30 to 1:30 Friday meeting.
	[Rahul Jain] Check Custom Line: Contact Akshai to check status and request timelines for custom line items feature (PDMs and PNS packages).
	[Nikhil Grover] Share App Documents: Send PRD, Figma designs, and documents detailing the new app overhaul/integration to Rahul Jain and Shubhangi for review.
	[Nikhil Grover] Schedule Integration Planning: Set up a dedicated discussion starting next week with Rahul Jain, Shubhangi, and Michael Bui to plan the new app integration.
	[Rahul Jain] Monitor Metrics: Monitor Osmos metrics after Monday 12:00 PM deployment. Check event numbers at 3:00 PM and 6:00 PM to proactively identify sales event abnormalities.
	[Nikhil Grover] Follow Up Invite: Follow up on the response status for the meeting invitation sent to Ashutosh.
	[Nikhil Grover] Provide GMP Data: Finalize and provide the GMP split data to Rachit Sachdeva by tomorrow.
	[Nikhil Grover] Complete Account Setup: Finish the setup process for the demand wise account tomorrow morning.
	[Nikhil Grover] Review Adoption Plan: Follow up with internal team regarding demand wise recommended journeys/adoption plan. Provide an update during the Monday afternoon call.
	Notes by Gemini

subject: Advertima / Open Project Items

messages.body:
Hi Nikhil,

Ahead of tomorrow’s meeting, I’d like to report back on four key items to make the best use of all our time.

Alerting
- We identified why alerting did not fire during the network outage.
- The CMS is running on a Fairprice-dedicated server instance, not our server, usually used for pilots.
- Because of this, standard out-of-the-box alerts were not active.
- The alerting setup is still being finalised with the CMS vendor.
- There is currently no ETA; follow-ups are ongoing and will be repeated daily if not resolved.

Sync / Store Rollout
- The plan was to identify a stable configuration that works with the store network without triggering power issues.
- A working configuration has not yet been identified.
- To progress safely, we now have mirrored identical players in Zurich.
- These players run on the same configuration as the FairPrice network.
- This allows testing under production-like conditions without risking the store network.
- Testing is ongoing, but there is no ETA at this point.
- This update will also have to merge with the next item, the heat fix

Heat Fix / Black Screens
- As a reminder, we suspect short-term black screens are heat-related.
- A fix limiting SSD I/O (to reduce temperature peaks) was deployed today on one device in Vivo
- We will monitor this device for 24 hours, covering a full shopping cycle.
- If successful, we will expand the fix to all screens within this store.
- After validation, it will be rolled out more broadly.

Maintenance Availability (CNY)
- ADE, the integrator responsible for physical installations and interventions, will be on Chinese New Year break.
- Break period: February 13th to February 23rd.
- During this time, there will be no physical intervention capability.
- This represents an operational risk.
- We should discuss tomorrow how this period will be handled.

Best,
Christian

--

Christian Bahrendt
Global Head of Customer Success & Projects
+49 176 24 11 85 45
christian.bahrendt@advertima.com

 

Advertima Vision AG
St.Gallen (HQ) | Zürich | London | Singapore | Dubai | US

Hi team,

Thanks for the time just now. Here are the meeting minutes:

Project ownership
Advertima owns the technical integration during the POV, including with TTD and other channel partners.
Alerts
Advertima will share a timeline update by today
Christian to share the list of alerts and trigger criteria by tomorrow
FPG will advise the recipient list for each alert
Maintenance Availability (CNY)
ADE is on a CNY break from Feb 13 to Feb 23
ADE is available for urgent issues such as (i) a black screen / no content displayed, or (ii) hardware issues.
Content update failures are not deemed critical for ADE intervention, and will be managed by Ad Ops.
Content sync on store screens
Advertima is testing the mirrored setup, they will revert with a solution and timeline by the end of the week.
Heat fix / Black screens
The single-screen deployment from yesterday has been stable.
Full network deployment is scheduled for 10pm today.
Please feel free to add any information I may have missed.

Thanks!
Nikhil

Dear FairPrice team,

a quick update on some of the open topics

@Allen Umali 

Adtrac/Analytics Onboarding
The onboarding session is still open
As CNY off days approach, we should aim to get something in the calendars with all the relevant stakeholders
I would suggest next Tue, Wed, or Thu, either 3 pm, 4 pm or 5 pm
Content Quality
As indicated, the content delivery does not always follow the specs (specific Frame Rates, Lengths, etc).
We provide feedback directly in the delivery process, so no intervention is required there yet
It seems the non-spec content is only FPG-own content or from local vendors; external AAA brand content usually works

@Michael Bui 

Alerts

The vendor has now confirmed to have the function available by Monday next week
Verified as an available alerting event is when players go offline
Recommendation is to send them to a central monitoring email address so individual receivers can more easily filter this as incoming mailbox traffic, but Fairprice can also add individual email addresses
Content sync on store screens

The verified working setting is now under investigation by the vendor
Advertima is pushing for feedback, which is central for an ETA to roll out to stores and will provide it as soon as it is received
Roll out then with pre-agreed timing and availabilities, as currently for the Heat fix

Black Screen
The black screen in AMK was successfully rectified by rebooting
The Advertima device itself was online and rebootable
However, despite software power cycling, apparently, the HDMI handshake with the display did not work
Research into root causes is ongoing; threads are the quality of the used USB-C to HDMI cables (a common suspect for HDMI handshake problems). 
A connection to the heat topic is plausible, which might have triggered the original HDMI handshake misfiring. A low probability angle is time desynchronisation that usually does not affect HDMI-related connections

Heat fix / Black screens
The deployed setup is still running stably
As AMK was the store with a black screen today and has been problematic in the past, we recommend updating AMK and NEX next at the beginning of next week and then scheduling the remaining stores as a last batch at a convenient time. 

Best regards,
Christian

Hi Michael,

These are the latest updates for our topics, also reflecting your WhatsApp message:

RCA & Heat Fix

Right now, we do not have an identified root cause yet
For this reason, we are applying the heat fix right now, as this is the first thing we want to exclude
To identify a root cause, we should complete the deployment, continuing with AMK and NEX. We could continue on Monday.
If this fixes it, it was the root cause.
If this does not fix the issue, we have to move to environmental factors in our analysis.

Screen Syncing

The setup has been reviewed and can be deployed
The identified setup sends a heartbeat package every half a second per device
The size of the UDP Data payload is 152 bytes. The total Ethernet package is 194 bytes
2 packages with information about the spot and other details are sent every 10 seconds.
The size of each Ethernet package is ~512 bytes, which is ~1kB in total
So, in total, this is per device ~29kB per minute or ~1,68MB/h, about ~17MB per 10h and ~33MB for a full day of 19h for the maximum opening hours
For the maximum case of 10 screens, like in JEM, this would be 1.68 × 10 × 9 ≈ 151 MB per hour → ≈ 2.9 GB over 19h on a store level. [ Total store ≈ 1.68 MB/h × n(n−1) ]
All traffic is local, expected to remain within the same subnet
To better control the scenario, we would start to deploy it in batches, starting with 2 screens, analogous to the heat fix.

Alerting

The email alert is ready from the CMS vendor side. We can send email alerts based on (a) offline players and (b) content playback errors.
Here, we need an email address to send it to. 
We recommend using a central email address and distributing from there. 
Set up would be immediate, with a 24h turnaround time.
…

In parallel, we are in the middle of externalising Advertima alerting signals.
The outbound alert is not yet available, but we have successfully established the following metrics we can track:
Ops Connectivity: Online Status, Sensor Health, Player Health
HW Temperature: Board, CPU, SSD, GPU
Based on the reported metrics and the delivery format, we could provide a first alert in about 3-4 weeks from the start of definition

Best regards,
Christian

The Developer’s Sandbox: Instead of high-level slides, we’re focusing on the technical building blocks you need today:

Engineering Reliability (RAG & Vector Search): Learn the architecture required to ground your agents in enterprise data for accurate, production-grade output.
Workflow Compression with Antigravity: Get hands-on with the latest GenAI tools to automate the repetitive parts of your development lifecycle.
Introduction to Gemini Enterprise: Witness a No-Code approach to unifying AI tools and deploying pre-built agents from our partner ecosystem to scale expertise across your business.
